Source   Edit  

Retreat and ROE Evaluation System

Implements Rules of Engagement evaluation, retreat decisions, and morale modifiers (Section 7.3.4, 7.3.5)

ROE Evaluation (Section 7.1.1 and 7.3.4)Multi-House Retreat Priority (Section 7.3.4)Combat Termination Check (Section 7.3.4)Round Progress TrackingRetreat Execution HelpersString formatting

Imports

types

Consts

roeThresholds = [(roe: 0, threshold: 0.0,
                  description: "Avoid all hostile forces"), (roe: 1,
    threshold: 999.0, description: "Engage only defenseless"), (roe: 2,
    threshold: 4.0, description: "Engage with 4:1 advantage"), (roe: 3,
    threshold: 3.0, description: "Engage with 3:1 advantage"), (roe: 4,
    threshold: 2.0, description: "Engage with 2:1 advantage"), (roe: 5,
    threshold: 1.5, description: "Engage with 3:2 advantage"), (roe: 6,
    threshold: 1.0, description: "Engage equal or inferior"), (roe: 7,
    threshold: 0.67, description: "Engage even if outgunned 3:2"), (roe: 8,
    threshold: 0.5, description: "Engage even if outgunned 2:1"), (roe: 9,
    threshold: 0.33, description: "Engage even if outgunned 3:1"), (roe: 10,
    threshold: 0.0, description: "Engage regardless of size")]
Source   Edit

    forbids: [].}
Pretty print retreat evaluation Source   Edit   
proc canRetreat(taskForce: TaskForce; roundNumber: int): bool {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if Task Force can retreat this round Section 7.3.5: Can only retreat after first round Source   Edit

                            consecutiveRoundsNoChange: int): tuple[
    shouldEnd: bool, reason: string, victor: Option[HouseId]] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if combat should end Returns (shouldEnd, reason, victor) Source   Edit   
proc evaluateRetreat(taskForce: TaskForce; allTaskForces: seq[TaskForce];
                     prestige: int): RetreatEvaluation {....raises: [ValueError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Evaluate whether Task Force wants to retreat Section 7.3.4: Multi-Faction Retreat Evaluation

Returns evaluation with decision and reasoning

Source   Edit   
proc executeRetreat(taskForces: var seq[TaskForce]; houseId: HouseId) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Remove Task Force from battle (retreat to fallback system) In actual game, this would trigger movement to fallback system For combat simulation, we just mark as retreated Source   Edit   
proc getMoraleROEModifier(prestige: int): int {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get ROE modifier based on House prestige/morale Section 7.3.4: Morale ROE Modifier table Source   Edit   
proc getRetreatPriority(taskForces: seq[TaskForce]): seq[HouseId] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Determine retreat order when multiple houses retreat simultaneously Section 7.3.4: weakest first, then by house ID Source   Edit   
proc hasProgressThisRound(roundResults: seq[RoundResult]): bool {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Check if any state changes occurred this round Used to detect stalemates Source   Edit
